argument_substitution.mechanism: $ARGUMENTS (all args), $ARGUMENTS[N] (Nth arg), $N (positional), ${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID} and other env vars; shared with skills argument substitution

builtin_commands.mechanism: extensive set of built-in slash commands (/help, /clear, /compact, /config, /model, /mcp, /memory, /plan, etc.) plus bundled-skill commands (/batch, /debug, /loop); not user-modifiable

providerExtensions.name: Command File Format (.md in .claude/commands/)
providerExtensions.description: Custom commands in Claude Code are Markdown files stored in .claude/commands/ (project scope) or ~/.claude/commands/ (user scope). Each file's name becomes the slash command name (e.g., .claude/commands/deploy.md becomes /deploy). The file content becomes the prompt that Claude executes. Commands work identically to skills; if a command and skill share the same name, the skill takes precedence. Custom commands support the same argument substitution as skills: $ARGUMENTS, $ARGUMENTS[N], $N, ${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}, ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}. The commands reference page notes that commands and skills are listed together and both types appear in the / command menu.

providerExtensions.name: Built-In Commands
providerExtensions.description: Claude Code ships approximately 60 built-in commands whose behavior is coded into the CLI. Representative built-ins: /add-dir (add working directory), /agents (manage agents), /autofix-pr (spawn web session watching a PR for CI failures), /btw (side question without adding to conversation), /clear (clear context), /color (set prompt bar color), /compact (compact conversation), /config (/settings, open settings interface), /context (visualize context usage), /copy (copy last response to clipboard), /cost (token usage stats), /debug (enable debug logging), /diff (interactive diff viewer), /doctor (diagnose installation), /effort (set model effort level), /export (export conversation as text), /hooks (read-only hook browser), /init (initialize CLAUDE.md), /insights (session analysis report), /install-github-app, /keybindings, /login, /logout, /mcp (manage MCP connections), /memory (browse/edit CLAUDE.md and auto-memory files), /model (select model), /permissions (/allowed-tools, manage permission rules), /plan (enter plan mode), /plugin, /reload-plugins, /remote-control, /rename (rename session), /resume (resume session by ID or name), /rewind (/checkpoint, rewind conversation), /sandbox, /schedule (cloud scheduled tasks), /security-review, /skills (list skills), /stats, /status, /statusline, /tasks (/bashes, manage background tasks), /teleport (/tp, pull web session to terminal), /theme, /ultraplan, /usage, /voice (push-to-talk dictation), /web-setup. Availability depends on platform, plan, and environment.

providerExtensions.name: Bundled Skill Commands
providerExtensions.description: Several commands are implemented as bundled skills rather than hard-coded CLI logic: /batch (orchestrate large-scale parallel changes across a codebase using background agents in isolated git worktrees; decomposes into 5-30 independent units, each opened as a PR), /claude-api (load Claude API reference for the project's language), /debug (enable debug logging and troubleshoot using session debug log), /loop (run a prompt repeatedly on an interval; /loop 5m /foo or self-paced), /simplify (review recently changed files for reuse and quality issues by spawning three parallel review agents). These use the same skill mechanism as user-created skills and can also be auto-invoked by Claude.

providerExtensions.name: MCP Prompt Commands
providerExtensions.description: MCP servers can expose prompts that appear as slash commands using the format /mcp__<servername>__<promptname>. These are dynamically discovered from connected servers; server and prompt names are normalized (spaces become underscores). Arguments are passed space-separated after the command. MCP prompt results are injected directly into the conversation.

providerExtensions.name: Command Scopes (project, user)
providerExtensions.description: Custom commands live in .claude/commands/ (project scope, shareable via version control) or ~/.claude/commands/ (user scope, available in all projects). Skills take precedence over commands when both share a name. Both scopes support symlinks. Path-scoped behavior matching (similar to .claude/rules/ paths frontmatter) is not a documented feature for commands; commands are global within their scope.
